document.title: lionscliapp Spec Addendum: read/write_json
document.purpose: Explicate how read_json and write_json shall work now. Overrules the initial spec.
implementation_notes: Path resolution delegated to get_path() in src/lionscliapp/paths.py

get_path.purpose: Resolve a path using the specified mode flag.
description: Central path resolution function used by read_json and write_json.
Exported as public API via lionscliapp.__init__.

c.name: configured
c.raises: KeyError if path.<id> is not declared in ctx.

p.name: project
p.meaning: Resolve relative to project root directory (<execroot>/<project_dir>).
p.note: Returns id unchanged if already absolute.

e.name: execution_root
e.note: Returns id unchanged if already absolute.

f.name: filesystem
f.meaning: Treat id as literal filesystem path (cwd-relative if not absolute).
f.note: Returns id unchanged if already absolute.

errors: ValueError if mode is not one of: c, p, e, f
KeyError if mode is 'c' and path.<id> is not in ctx

write_json.signature: write_json(id: str, data: any, mode: str = 'c') -> None
write_json.purpose: Serialize data as JSON and write it to a filesystem path resolved via get_path().
description: write_json resolves the target path using get_path(id, path_mode).
The mode string may include one path resolution flag and optionally one formatting flag.
Path resolution semantics are owned by get_path().
Formatting flags affect JSON serialization only and do not influence path resolution.

constraints: Exactly one path flag must be present.
At most one formatting flag may be present.
Unknown or duplicate flags raise ValueError.

p.name: project
p.meaning: Resolve relative to project root directory.

f.name: filesystem
f.meaning: Treat id as literal filesystem path (cwd-relative if not absolute).

0.name: compact
0.meaning: Serialize JSON using compact formatting (no whitespace, separators=(',', ':')).

2.name: pretty_indent_2
2.meaning: Serialize JSON using pretty printing with indent=2.

formatting_resolution.description: Formatting is determined by a priority chain depending on mode and flags.
priority: 1. Explicit format flag (0 or 2) always takes precedence.
2. For 'c' mode without format flag: check ctx['json.indent.<id>'] (0 means compact).
3. For non-'c' modes without format flag: use DEFAULT_INDENT (2).

json.indent.<id>.meaning: Indentation level. 0 means compact (no whitespace); positive values indent with that many spaces.

behavior: Parse mode string into path_flag and optional format_flag.
Determine formatting via priority chain (format_flag > ctx keys > default).
Serialize data using json.dumps with determined formatting.
errors: TypeError if data is not JSON-serializable.
KeyError if mode is 'c' and path.<id> is not declared.
usage_examples: write_json('inventory', data, 'c')  # configured path, formatting from ctx or default
design_note: Formatting shorthand is intentionally limited to {0,2}.
Additional formatting styles are explicitly out of scope for v0.
ctx keys are only honored for 'c' mode to keep behavior predictable for direct paths.

read_json.purpose: Read and deserialize JSON from a filesystem path resolved via get_path().
description: read_json resolves the path using get_path(id, path_flag).
Formatting flags are invalid for read_json and raise ValueError if present.

constraints: Exactly one path flag must be present.
Formatting flags (0, 2) are invalid for read_json and raise ValueError.

behavior: Parse mode string and validate no format flags present.
errors: ValueError if mode contains format flags or is otherwise invalid.
KeyError if mode is 'c' and path.<id> is not declared.
FileNotFoundError if path does not exist.
json.JSONDecodeError if file contains invalid JSON.

DEFAULT_INDENT.purpose: Default indentation level when no format flag is specified and ctx keys are not configured.
